skill.name: d3-viz
skill.description: Create custom D3.js charts, interactive SVG visualizations, network diagrams, geographic views, and framework-ready chart components.
skill.summary: Build bespoke D3.js data visualizations with scales, layouts, interactions, transitions, and reusable templates.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives caused by JavaScript template literals, Markdown code fences, and D3 visualization terms. No prompt injection, credential access, filesystem access, command execution, or data exfiltration evidence was found. A medium client-side DOM injection risk exists because the interactive tooltip renders data-derived values with HTML.

medium_findings.title: Data-Derived Tooltip HTML Injection
medium_findings.description: The interactive chart template writes tooltip HTML from data fields. If labels or categories come from untrusted data, crafted values could inject markup into the page. Use text nodes or sanitize values before rendering HTML.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code uses D3 .html() with interpolated data fields in the tooltip. This is a known DOM injection pattern when visualization data is user-controlled.

low_findings.title: External CDN Dependency in Setup Guidance
low_findings.description: The setup instructions include loading D3 from the public d3js.org CDN. This is normal for examples but depends on external network availability and third-party script integrity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is clearly a D3 CDN script tag in documentation. It is not exfiltration, but it is an external script dependency.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTML Rendering From Visualization Data
dangerous_patterns.description: The tooltip uses HTML rendering for fields such as label, category, and size. This should be replaced with safe text rendering for untrusted datasets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly visible in the interactive template. The risk depends on whether caller-provided data is trusted.

content.value_statement: Standard chart libraries often limit custom layouts and interactions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create precise D3.js visualizations for complex data.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use D3.js instead of simpler chart libraries.
Provides patterns for scales, axes, margins, labels, and SVG layout.
Includes React templates for basic and interactive D3 charts.
Covers hierarchical, network, geographic, time series, and categorical visualizations.
Recommends color schemes and accessibility checks for data visualization.
Guides transitions, zoom, pan, hover, click, and tooltip interactions.
limitations: It provides guidance and templates, not a packaged charting runtime.
Generated visualizations still need testing with real data and target browsers.
The examples assume D3.js knowledge for deeper customization.
Tooltip HTML must be sanitized when data can contain untrusted text.

use_cases.title: Product Analytics Dashboards
use_cases.description: Create custom funnel, retention, and cohort views when standard dashboard charts cannot show the required interaction.

use_cases.title: Editorial Data Stories
use_cases.description: Build publication-quality graphics with precise labels, color choices, annotations, and responsive SVG behavior.

use_cases.title: Network and Hierarchy Exploration
use_cases.description: Design force graphs, trees, treemaps, and other structured views for complex relationship data.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the d3-viz skill to create a simple bar chart for this dataset. Include scales, axes, labels, and accessible colors.

prompt_templates.title: Add Interactions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the d3-viz skill to add hover tooltips, click selection, and smooth transitions to my scatter plot.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Specialized Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the d3-viz skill to design a force-directed network diagram with grouped nodes, labels, legend, and zoom controls.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate With a Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the d3-viz skill to convert this visualization idea into a React component with safe data binding and responsive sizing.

output_examples.input: Create an interactive scatter plot for customer segments.
output_examples.output: A React D3 component plan with scales, point sizing, category colors, tooltip behavior, selected point state, and accessibility notes.

output_examples.input: Show product revenue by region over time.
output_examples.output: A time series visualization approach with date parsing, line generation, axes, legend placement, responsive dimensions, and color recommendations.
A force graph design with node grouping, link weights, drag behavior, zoom controls, labels, and performance considerations.

best_practices: Start with the data structure, then choose scales, layout, and interactions.
Use text rendering or sanitization for labels from untrusted datasets.
Test color contrast, keyboard access, responsiveness, and browser performance.
anti_patterns: Do not use D3.js when a simple static chart library is enough.
Do not mix framework rendering and D3 DOM updates without clear ownership.
Do not render untrusted labels through tooltip HTML without sanitization.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build custom D3.js charts, SVG visualizations, network diagrams, maps, and interactive data experiences.

faq.question: Can it work with React?
faq.answer: Yes. The included templates show React patterns using refs, effects, state, and D3 rendering.

faq.question: Does it replace chart libraries?
faq.answer: No. It is best for custom visualizations that need more control than standard chart components provide.

faq.question: Does it include sample data?
faq.answer: Yes. The assets include sample time series, categorical, scatter, hierarchy, network, geographic, and diverging data.

faq.question: What security concern should users know?
faq.answer: Avoid rendering untrusted data through tooltip HTML. Use text rendering or sanitize values first.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
